summary: Location-based platform connecting local workers with nearby job providers (simplified): users create profiles, job providers post jobs, and the system matches workers to jobs. Tech stack: Motoko backend + React frontend; no external map/GPS services.
architectural_notes: Use Motoko for backend canisters and React for the frontend.
Avoid external map/GPS services; implement simplified locality/nearby logic without third-party geolocation APIs.

existing_features.title: Implement Internet Identity sign-in and use the authenticated Principal as the account identifier for all user-owned data (profiles, job postings, and applications/matches).
Add worker and job-provider profile management: create, view, and edit basic profile information, including an area field (e.g., city/region/postcode) used for local matching (no GPS/maps).
Implement job posting functionality for Providers: create, view, edit, close/reopen, and delete job listings; each job includes title, description, area, category/tags, and status.

Implement an area-based matching flow between Workers and Jobs without external map/GPS: workers can apply to jobs; providers can review applicants and accept/reject; the system highlights ‘local’ matches when worker area and job area match (exact match or simple normalized match).

Create a coherent, consistent UI theme for the app (colors, typography, spacing, and component styling) suitable for a local marketplace, and apply it across all pages (avoid blue/purple as the primary palette).

Build the main frontend screens and navigation: landing/browse jobs, authentication entry, profile (view/edit), provider job management, job detail + apply, provider applicant review, and worker application status.

new_feature_requests.summary: User profiles for workers and job providers (create/view/edit basic profile info)
Job posting functionality for providers (create/view/manage job listings)
Matching flow between job listings and workers (nearby/local matching without external map/GPS services)
Motoko backend + React frontend implementation constraint

Persist all core data in the Motoko canister (profiles, jobs, applications) and expose a minimal, typed API to the frontend for CRUD operations and queries (lists/detail views), following the single-actor backend constraint.

projectName: Local job matching platform: profiles, job postings, and area-based matching
